metadata.method_A: Pure Claude (best economic reasoning, no Abel framework)
metadata.method_B: Claude + Abel (Markov blanket causal structure from real API calls)

six_step_workflow.step_1_classify: All 20 questions classified as FOMC monetary policy -> direct_graph through federalFunds/inflationRate
six_step_workflow.step_2_hypotheses: For each: hawkish (inflation concern), dovish (weakness concern), neutral (mechanism description), + contrarian
six_step_workflow.step_3_screen: Mapped text to Abel graph nodes: federalFunds (20 blanket members), inflationRate (20 members), GDP (20 members), unemploymentRate (20 members)
six_step_workflow.step_4_observe: Used Markov blanket causal paths for disambiguation: inflationRate->federalFunds, GDP->federalFunds, unemploymentRate<->GDP
six_step_workflow.step_5_web: N/A for historical FOMC text classification (no freshness-sensitive claims)
six_step_workflow.step_6_synthesize: Score-based synthesis weighting Abel causal path strengths

federalFunds.description: Effective or target federal funds rate used as the core U.S. overnight policy benchmark. It anchors short-term financial conditions and transmits monetary policy into broader credit, rate, and demand channels.

inflationRate.description: Rate of change in the general price level over time.

GDP.description: Broad activity benchmark for aggregate output, growth, and business-cycle conditions.

unemploymentRate.description: Share of the labor force that is unemployed but actively seeking work.

key_causal_relationships: inflationRate <-> federalFunds (bidirectional; inflation causes rate hikes)
GDP -> federalFunds (weak GDP causes rate cuts)
